response.justification: This candidate is a full-stack generalist engineer with strong software fundamentals but lacks the mandatory requirements for this role. With only ~6 months of professional C++ experience (not the required 5-10 years), no demonstrable Windows platform development, and zero capital markets or financial services domain expertise, they fall far short of the position's core requirements. While their CI/CD knowledge and communication skills are strengths, they cannot fulfill the technical stack or domain needs.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software engineering practices with TDD, testing focus, and engineering rigor across multiple languages and projects
Solid CI/CD and DevOps experience including GitLab administration, deployment pipelines, and Docker containerization
Excellent communication and knowledge-sharing abilities evidenced by workshops, talks, and open-source contributions
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience (National Instruments) versus the required 5-10 years; recent work focuses on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
No evidence of Windows platform development; career has been primarily Linux/web-based with no mention of Windows expertise
Complete absence of capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services domain experience across entire career
